text: SQL injection attacks exploit unsanitized user input in database queries, allowing attackers to read, modify, or delete data.
Broken authentication and session management allows attackers to compromise passwords, keys, or session tokens to assume other users identities.
Cross-site scripting (XSS) flaws occur when an application includes unvalidated or unescaped user input in its output, enabling script injection.
Insecure direct object references occur when an application exposes internal implementation objects such as files, directories, or database keys directly without access control.
Security misconfiguration is the most commonly seen issue, including insecure default configurations, incomplete setup, open cloud storage, and verbose error messages.
Man-in-the-middle attacks allow adversaries to intercept and alter communications between two parties who believe they are communicating directly with each other.
Denial of service attacks flood a target system with traffic or requests to exhaust its resources and prevent legitimate users from accessing the service.
